summary: Exercised dark mode end-to-end in a real headless Chrome (bundle installed; stale chromedriver bypassed so Selenium Manager fetched a matching driver). The committed system test passes (7 assertions: toggle light->dark->light, immediate apply, aria-pressed reflects state, cookie persistence across visits), and a temporary CDP-driven verification confirmed the subtler requirements: R1 defaults to system=dark with no cookie, R10 follows an OS scheme change to dark when no manual choice exists (matchMedia 'change' listener fires and toggles the .dark class after the event settles), and R11 keeps a manual dark choice when the OS later flips to light. Static checks cover the rest: theme toggle renders in both app layouts (sidebar for all authenticated pages, auth layout for sign-in) satisfying R3; a scan of app/views + helpers found zero hardcoded light-only colors (bg-white/bg-gray/text-gray-900/border-gray) — the whole app repaints via semantic tokens redeclared under .dark, satisfying R12; and status_badge always renders the status word alongside color with dark: ring/text variants, satisfying R13. All 13 requirements verified; no defects found.
